item.title: this is so messed up, yet totally believable
item.content:encoded:
not counting any anomalous properties, I totally believe a morally ambiguous MegaCorp. (especially an interdimensional one like LCE) would think this was a good idea, and that's the scary thing.
so far loving everything I'm reading about LCE. Enough so, in fact, that I shared the "Hideaway Home" skip with some friends on discord, described LCE in a nutshell, and then followed it up with "by the way, they're selling earth".

edit: so wait, the impossible-to-escape home makes sense, but what made them at the homicidal creature? Unless that was a horrifically-mutated resident or something?

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The first image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

The second image belongs to flah. As it is under a CC BY-NC-SA 2.0 license, it is not compatible with CC BY-SA 3.0 and has since been removed.
Who did this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You made a mistake, you linked to 2935 which is "O, Death" on the "for more info" part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there supposed to be two Addendums 1740-B?
… And not 1740-A and 1740-B?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reallly imho you should remove the badly placed GOI here.

I'm not fan of the other GOIs like that anyway (like that alex university). And the end of the articla can just be cut of. Wich would improve the Quality of the article. downvote for now.

item.title: Adolescents of less than 13 years in age?
item.content:encoded:
Hmm… did the Light Couriers intend to send the Prison Break Playground to this universe? What happens if you ask an instance of SCP-1940-1 about the Playground?

Also… :

"human adolescent of less than 13 years in age"

Aren't most adolescents more than 13 years in age… do you mean to say 'children', as implied by having the plaque say:

"Intended for usage on children 3 to 13 years of age"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about the slicing off of the finger? That must reaaaaally hurt! And it was intentional: did the plaque explain that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Many injuries caused by "Bouncy Guards" are purely accidental.

So much horror and funny in that one sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subjects will also be provided with water and meals by SCP-1740-1, and are currently produced through unknown means.

The wording here is just… off.
I tried to reword it in several ways but none of them worked.
Also "through" was misspelled as "trough". I feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Pretty sure the Foundation Ethics Committee would not approve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who said they'd be resistant ? They'll just be emotionally prepared.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww… the red hippo was way creepier than the new one. :(

item.title: Image Change
item.content:encoded:
Realized the second image is from a copyrighted picture, deleted it and replaced it with this one, which is under Creative Commons.

First image is from a news article, also.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll work on it as soon as I can (later today).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Prison camp training-play is a fun concept, and I particularly like the guillotine to punish escape attempts. 1 cm is just enough to be WTF! awful while still mild enough to be a reasonable educational negative reinforcement. "Reasonable" by alternate reality gulag dystopia standards, at least.

But the phrasing here is very awkward. It not only comes across as poor tone, but is jarring to read. Pretty much every sentence featuring the word "though" needs to be re-written as two sentences, for example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"has made spawn, must put in stasis"

This induced giggles in me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To prepare your child for an event such as capturination, slavery, and spaghettification,

I'm intrigued that this experience will render a child resistant to the tidal forces of black holes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I see what you mean now. Makes sense!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll fix the typo, but I don't recall there being a sign telling them what happens when they sit on the bench. Maybe you confused it with the containment procedures?

Oh, you meant the bronze plaque. The goo thing is a common thing from where this came from, so it's not like they're being malicious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad at all and worth an upvote. I did have a question about this sentence, however:

"Subjects will also be provided with water and meals by SCP-1740-1, though are produced through unknown means."

There's a typo here, but I'm not sure what you meant. Did you want "…that are produced through unknown means"? Or "These are produced through unknown means"?

Another suggestion would be for the sign not to actually tell the parents about the "sticky goo" because who'd willingly sign up for that? Instead a veiled reference to them being able to "take a break" or "sit this one out" would make it a little more menacing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of like the idea of this being made by Wondertainment to train kids for being in stressful situations. But Wondertainment thinks of itself as nice. It wouldn't cut bits off people's fingers. Not permanently, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't say I'm a fan of SCPs that come with disclaimers on them, and nothing about the rest of this is doing it for me.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the thought you put into this. And it adds a wonderful creep factor because the bench is acting on a metaphysical idea, not a simple "has made spawn, must put in stasis" reaction. This means they have some manner of technology or magic or what have you to recognize whether a person considers themselves to be a parent. Think about those implications. And the mention of producing a stronger generation, to better face fear? What horrible place did this come from where this is not only apparently mundane, but accepted and expected?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would if I hadn't just gotten drunk. Apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My mind, she is the boggle. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I assume the kids that get their toes lopped off piece by piece get sent back to prison to try again, right?

This would be correct.

Also, is being stuck in permanent stasis an intended side effect from sitting on the bench and unknowingly having an illegitimate child, or that's just an oversight?

They would have to recognize the fact that they have a living child, or else they aren't really a parent.

Shouldn't the bench's properties apply to other manner of supervisors(teachers, sitters, other relatives), as well?

I'd say the people who made this didn't really have this in mind, as it was actually made more for themselves, if anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This idea is fun. I would attach them to some sort of edutainment manufacturers with spartan inclinations(and a properly referential name), but that's neither here nor there.

I assume the kids that get their toes lopped off piece by piece get sent back to prison to try again, right? Or are they forcefully released and forbidden to come back as they are "dead" and out of the game now?

Also, is being stuck in permanent stasis an intended side effect from sitting on the bench and unknowingly having an illegitimate child, or that's just an oversight? Shouldn't the bench's properties apply to other manner of supervisors(teachers, sitters, other relatives), as well?

All in all, I gotta agree that being accidentally trampled to death by bouncy plastic hippos is probably the least worst way a kid can die by things in this site. Also, pretty amusing as mental images go, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

I sort of liked this until I got to the parent/bench piece. Then I read the plaque, and really, I just don't like it at all. I think this would be MUCH better/creepier if they did the animal crazies did their thing without any explanation. But I appear to be the minority.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed the qualifications for SCP-1740-A to simply being a parent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We don't. :P

But honestly, it's not like I'm trying to make it big like all the other GOIs; It's something I'm doing for fun.

actually, I'm not sure what else there is to say in them. :/

Never mind, I'll add to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The term "virgin" means different things depending on who you ask, and most of the definitions that I can think of are sort of in a grey area outside of heterosexual activity — and that's just in America; other countries, I'm sure, have entirely different paradigms. The Foundation would define the specific activity or activities that disqualify a person from the effect. No vote at the moment 'cause I haven't finished reading, but I'd probably be inclined to not vote until that's fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at "being beaten to death by plastic bouncy hippos." Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why do we need another GOI that makes weird things? Why couldn't this be Factory, or Wondertainment? And the containment procedures are REALLY short, they need better detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing you're talking about the sentence structure of my writing, since I know it ain't really good. Care to point out some things?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great idea, but the article really, really, really needs lots of editing. Not just typos and agreement, but tonal issues as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OK. I saw the pictures and thought I wasn't going to like this; started reading along and thinking "Hmm, that's pretty weird." Then the LCE bit both tied it all together and got me laughing.

Spaghettification? No thanks, I'd prefer to be linguinified or fusilliated. These guys need to get their pasta in order.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: K, I'll try to fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah-ha, that makes sense. It might stand to clarify that slightly in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, no actually. Though that could be cool.

All I was thinking was that they kept them underneath the platforms on the playground. Like, in this picture, if this were the SCP, they would be kept underneath the stairs to the slide and such.

The things who made this playground aren't the smartest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
0_o… Wow. that's evil. i love guillotine. This whole thing is wonderfully dark and hillarious.

one concern:

instances of SCP-1740-1 will escort the subject underneath SCP-1740

Where underneath? is there a cave system? or is it some structure of the playground? Oooh… is it under one of those dome structures? Like this? Those always seemed a little cage-like to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Altered that line. And I'm happy you like LCE. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoy the Light Courier Enterprises stuff. This and our strange university colleagues in the universe next door are my favorite alternate reality organizations.

This was a little awkward: "will be forcefully escorted by 5 to four instances"

But other than that, hearty upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the sheer factor of using something as creepy as a playground. Also like the tone, very well written. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, it's not.

To clarify, it shouldn't be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
is this a word?
capturination

in the sentence "slavery, capturination, spaghettification"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh good, I was getting visions of a coma you couldn't bring someone out of without very, very unethical means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On its own it doesn't offer too much, but the implications of the Light Courier group are bizarre enough to make you question everything you might have ignored earlier. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing happens. I'll explain that in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …what happens when a non-virgin over the age of 18 sits on one of the benches… and doesn't have a child?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a real thing, yo: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spaghettification

Playground prisons, yay +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
spaghettification

brb laughing to death

Seriously though, this is pretty cool, and I'm pretty sure I'll never look at those bouncy animals as trustfully ever again. Take this +1.

item.title: For Dmatix's challenge.
item.content:encoded:
Thanks goes to Longwayhome for reviewing.

The words in question for the challenge were playground, number, saliva, prison, guillotine.

Also, this is my third SCP with Light Courier Enterprises. yaaaaaaay

If you want more LCE, follow the links or read the tale.
